  • payphone.

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.​

Ipswich Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Ipswich railway station is located underneath the Ipswich Health Plaza, between Bell Street to the west and East Street in the east. It services trains travelling westbound to Charleville and back into Brisbane Roma Street station, as well as trains in the South East Queensland (SEQ) network. The station has four platforms across two island platforms, with tracks on each side. Platforms 1 and 2 are to the south and 3 and 4 are to the north. The station can be accessed via Bell Street, where lifts and ramps offer access to the platforms. There is a carpark, including accessible spaces off Bell Street. There is another carpark which is accessed from Olga Street. 

Inside the entrance of the Ipswich Health Plaza there is: 

  • customer information screens
  • customer service window including ticketing for long-distance trains 
  • hearing loop
  • ticketing and Go-card top-up machine (SEQ services only)
  • vending machines.

You will find several facilities on the platforms for your convenience.

Platforms 1 and 2:​​​

  • customer information screens
  • customer seating
  • payphone 
  • toilets.

Platforms 3 and 4:​

  • customer information screens
  • customer seating
  • drink fountain
  • next train information 
  • payphone
  • toilets (accessible).

Bus stops and taxi bays are located next to the station on Bell Street, providing connections to various local destinations. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.​

There is one platform, which is on the eastern side of the track. There is a carparking area in front of the station. Access to the platform is via a ramp or steps.  

There is customer seating and shelter.

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you require assistance boarding, please inform our staff at the time of booking, and we will be happy to assist you.

Landsborough Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Landsborough railway station is located between Cribb Street on the western side and Maleny Street to the east. The station has two platforms that service trains travelling southbound to Brisbane and northbound to Bundaberg and Cairns, as well as trains on the South East Queensland (SEQ) network. Platform 1 is on the western side and Platform 2 is on the eastern side. There is carparking on both sides of the station, including accessible spaces. There are lifts available to access the platforms as well as stairs and ramps. An overpass bridge offers access to the platforms. 

You will find the following facilities on the platform. Most facilities are accessed on Platform 1, though Platform 2 has a help phone as well. 

  • bike lockers
  • customer service window (including long-distance ticket sales)
  • customer seating
  • hearing loop
  • help phone
  • payphone
  • toilet (accessible and with a baby change table)
  • ticketing and Go-card top-up machine (SEQ services only).

Bus stops are located on either sides of the station, providing connections to various local destinations. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.​

Caboolture Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Caboolture railway station is located between Matthew Terrace to the west and Walter Street to the east. It services trains travelling southbound to Brisbane and northbound to Cairns, as well as trains in the South East Queensland (SEQ) network. The station has three platforms: one side platform (Platform 1) on the western side and one island platform (Platforms 2 and 3) on the eastern side of the station. There are lifts and stairs to the bridge that connects the platforms at the northern end of the station. 

There is car parking available (including accessible carparks) on the Matthew Terrace and Walter Street sides of the station. There are also drop off zones on both sides. There is a bus stop and taxi bay on the western side of the station.  ?

You will find several facilities on the platforms for your convenience.

Platform 1​​​​​

  • customer information screen (SEQ network)
  • customer seating
  • customer service window (long distance ticketing included) with a hearing loop
  • help phone
  • lifts
  • luggage check-in
  • next train information
  • payphone 
  • toilets (sharps bin)
  • ticketing and Go-card top-up Machine (SEQ services)
  • stairs.

Platform 2​​​​​

  • accessible toilet
  • customer seating
  • help phone
  • lifts 
  • next train information
  • stairs
  • vending machine.

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.​

Bundaberg Station and Travel Centre.texttranscriptstring:
Bundaberg railway station is located between McLean Street to the east, Burrum Street to the west, and Bourbong Street to the north. The station has one main platform that serves trains travelling north to Rockhampton and Cairns, south to Brisbane and west to Longreach. The station can be accessed via McLean Street from the carpark, which provides both a ramp and steps for access. The carpark is the primary entry point to the station and has accessible space. 

There is a Travel Centre located at Bundaberg railway station, where you can purchase long-distance train tickets. There are also luggage trolleys and scales and the luggage check-in service. This is to the left when you enter the station. 

You will also find the following facilities on the platform: 

  • customer seating and shelter
  • toilets, with accessible option, baby change facilities, and a sharps bin 
  • a payphone for public use
  • next train information
  • luggage trolleys
  • a help phone for assistance
  • vending machines 
  • a hearing loop.

There is a bus stop on McLean Street for onward journeys. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.

Corinda Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Corinda railway station is located between Watt Street and Aidan’s Way on the western side and Browne Street on the eastern side. It services trains travelling westbound to Charleville and back into Brisbane Roma Street station, as well as trains on the South East Queensland (SEQ) network. The station has five platforms which can be accessed via lifts or stairs on Aidan’s Way or a lift or ramp on Browne Street. There is a car park, including accessible spaces, on the Browne Street side. There are also drop off zones on both sides (Watt Street on the western side) and bus and taxi zones on the eastern side.  

You will find the following facilities on the platforms.

Platforms 1 and 2 (Browne Street side):​​​

  • customer seating 
  • hearing loop
  • help phone
  • next train information.

Platforms 2, 3 and 4 (middle):​

  • customer seating
  • hearing loop
  • help phone
  • next train information
  • vending machine (platforms 3 and 4 only).

Platform 5 (Watt Street and Aidan’s Way side):​

  • help phone
  • next train information.

The bridge over the platforms has:​​

  • customer information screens
  • customer service window (including ticketing for long-distance trains) with hearing loop
  • drink fountain
  • help phone
  • ticketing and Go-card top-up machine (SEQ services) 
  • toilet (including accessible and a sharps bin).

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.​

Cairns Station and Travel Centre.texttranscriptstring:
Cairns railway station is located in the Cairns Central Shopping Centre, on the block surrounded by McLeod, Aplin, Bunda and Spence Streets. The station is located parallel to Bunda Street. The car park is in the shopping centre and is accessed via Aplin or Spence Streets. There is accessible parking near the railway station entrance on the Platform 1 side.  

The station has two platforms, which are connected via a footbridge, and can be accessed via steps or lifts. The lifts are located near the main entries. Platform 1 is closest to the carpark access. It has the Travel Centre where long distance and Kuranda Scenic Railway tickets can be purchased. Platform 1 is used for the Spirit of Queensland which travels between Cairns and Brisbane. Kuranda Scenic Railway departs from Platform 2 on the Bunda Street side. You can also purchase your Kuranda Scenic Railway ticket on the day of travel between 8.00am and 9.30am at the dedicated ticket office on Platform 2. 

On both platforms at Cairns railway station, you will find the following facilities:

  • customer seating
  • drink fountains 
  • hearing loops in the areas adjacent to the main entries
  • toilets, including an accessible option and baby change facilities, and
  • vending machines.

Additional amenities on Platform 1 include:

  • a payphone
  • luggage trolleys and a
  • help phone.

You can access Cairns railway station via the bus stops and taxi zone on Spence and McLeod Streets.

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.

Kuranda Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Kuranda railway station is located on Coondoo Street. It services the Kuranda Scenic Railway between Kuranda and Cairns. There are two platforms, which are on an island with tracks on each side. There is carparking available, including accessible spaces. The Kuranda area is quite hilly, with an uphill walk from the station into the town. The platforms are accessible via stairs or a lift. 

Upon arrival at Kuranda railway station, you will find several facilities for your convenience, including:

  • customer seating
  • hearing loop
  • payphone
  • shelter
  • ticketing office
  • toilets (accessible and including a baby change table).

There is a tearoom on the platform which is open daily and offers refreshments, snacks and souvenirs for purchase.

Skyrail Rainforest Cableway and buses can be accessed at street level above the station, via Arara Street, offering connections to various local destinations. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you require assistance boarding, please inform our team when booking and we will be happy to assist you. A hydraulic lift is available to board the carriage. ​

Rosewood Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Rosewood railway station is located between Railway Street to the north and Mill Street to the south. It services trains travelling westbound to Charleville and back into Brisbane Roma Street station, as well as trains in the South East Queensland (SEQ) network. The station has two platforms, opposite each other, with two tracks running down the middle. Platform 1 is on the Railway Street side and Platform 2 is on the Mill Street side. The station can be accessed from Railway Street, where a car park, including accessible spaces, is available. ?There are stairs and an overpass bridge connecting the platforms. There are no lifts at Rosewood station.

You will find most facilities on the side of Platform 1, including: 

  • bike lockers
  • help phone
  • customer seating
  • customer service window
  • payphone
  • ticketing and Go-card top-up machine (SEQ services only)
  • toilets (accessible).

Platform 2 also has customer seating and a help phone. 

A bus stop and taxi bay are located next to the station, providing connections to various local destinations. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.

Brisbane Railway Station (Roma Street).ahrnote:
​Additional Station Information​

Departures and arrivals for all Queensland Rail Travel services are from Platform 10.

Coffee Shop temporarily unavailable until further notice.

​Platform 10 - DAY OF TRAVEL TICKETING ONLY. Priority will be given to servicing day of travel. The Ticket office will open 90mins before the train departs and closes 30mins prior to the trains departure.​

Changes at Brisbane Roma Street Platform 10 

To accommodate the Cross River Rail project, the Brisbane Coach Terminal has moved to Parkland Crescent adjacent to the long-distance train platform (Platform 10).

The Roma Street parking and set down/pick up zones near Platform 10 are shared by coach terminal and train customers. 

Due to the increased activity in this area, we recommend customers allow more time and plan or book parking ahead of time.

A map showing passenger set down and pick up zones is shown below. For coach travel information, please contact your coach operator directly. The Department of Transport and Main Roads (TMR) is responsible for operating the coach terminal and further information can be found on their Queensland Government web page.
​

Set down and pick up zones​

Set down and pick up zones are on Parkland Boulevard with access to the station via escalators and a lift.

Three accessible set down/pick up bays, and two taxi bays are available on Parkland Crescent outside Platform 10.​

Extended customer parking

Extended parking is available at:
  • The Brisbane City Council operated College Close car park
  • The Cornerstone car park on Parkland Crescent
  • PLEASE NOTE: The Brisbane Transit Centre (BTC) carpark, operated by Secure Car Parking is now permanently closed
Car parking rates are set by operators and we recommend customers check the rates ahead of arrival.

Information about carparking is available on the CRR Brisbane Transit Centre webpage.

Replacement coaches for Queensland Rail Travel Services

Coaches replacing Queensland Rail Travel Network services will operate out of the Brisbane Coach Terminal that is located at the parklands side of Roma Street station.

Any customers joining replacement coach services at Brisbane Roma Street should wait at Platform 10 where they will be escorted to the Brisbane Coach Terminal by the onboard team.

Please continue to check the Service Updates page prior to departure for updates.​​​

Brisbane Railway Station (Roma Street).texttranscriptstring:
Roma Street railway station is a major station in Brisbane City and the wider Queensland Rail network. Platform 10 is where the long-distance trains typically depart and arrive and is on the Parkland Crescent side of the station, to the north.

There is a drop off zone at the front of the station for people with a disability. The Brisbane Coach Terminal is located across the road from the station. Paid parking for more than just a quick drop off is available under the Parklands apartments past the coach terminal. Access to the platform from the front of the station is flat. From the subway which has access to other local train platforms, access to Platform 10 is via a travellator, lift or stairs. 

On the platform, at the eastern end, there is an assisted travel boarding point, Travel Centre, luggage check-in facilities, luggage scales and a lift. Male and female toilets and a unisex accessible toilet with a baby change facility are in the middle of the platform. Sheltered seating and customer information screens are located along the length of the platform. There is a hearing loop on the platform for station announcements. An air-conditioned customer lounge with tables and chairs, a drink fountain and multiple power points, is in the middle of the platform, past the toilets.

There is a help phone and a taxi phone towards the exit to the bus station. There is a payphone in the subway. Tactile indicators are installed along the edges of the platform. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.

Freshwater Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Freshwater railway station is located on Kamerunga Road. It can also be accessed from Meller Street to the back. It services the Kuranda Scenic Railway between Cairns and Kuranda. There is one platform, which is on the Kamerunga Road side of the station. Also on Kamerunga Road, there is a drop off zone for people with disability and accessible carparking. Most of the parking is on Meller Street. Access to the platform is via a ramp.

Upon arrival at the Freshwater railway station, you will find several facilities for your convenience, including:

  • customer seating
  • drink fountain
  • hearing loop
  • payphone
  • shelter
  • ticketing office
  • toilet (accessible and with baby change table).

There is also a gift shop and café at the eastern end of the station. At the other end, there is an historical cottage (Redlynch Cottage) which you can tour before or after your journey and lush green gardens. Also at the eastern end is the coach parking and a shelter for customers transitioning between transport. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you require assistance boarding, please inform our team and we will be happy to assist you. A hydraulic lift is available to board the carriage. ​

Nambour Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Nambour railway station is located between Price Street to the north and Civic Way to the south. The station has an island platform, with train tracks on each side. It services trains travelling southbound to Brisbane, northbound to Rockhampton and Cairns, and west to Longreach. It is also used by passenger trains on the South East Queensland (SEQ) network. The station has a drop off zone on Civic Way, or car parking, including accessible spaces, off Price Street. ? From the carpark on Price Street, the station is accessed through a tunnel under the track. You can get to the tunnel via stairs or a lift. 

Platform 1 is on the northern side of the station, while Platform 2 is on the southern side. You will find the following facilities on the platform: 

  • bike lockers 
  • customer information screen 
  • customer seating
  • customer service window (including long-distance ticket sales)
  • drink fountain
  • hearing loop
  • next train information
  • toilet (accessible and with a baby change table).
  • ticketing and Go-card top-up machine (SEQ services only).

A bus stop and taxi bay are located next to the station on Civic Way, providing connections to various local destinations. 

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you need assistance boarding, please let our staff know at the time of booking, and we will be happy to help.

You will find the following facilities on the platform:

Laidley Railway Station.texttranscriptstring:
Laidley railway station can be accessed from Pike Street on the southside or Cribb Street on the northside of the station. It services passenger trains travelling between Charleville in the west and Brisbane Roma Street station in the east. Access to the platform is via a ramp or steps. There is parking available on the Pike Street side at the front of the station. The access from Cribb Street is via a pedestrian path.

Platform 1 can be accessed from the Pike Street side and is for trains travelling west. Platform 2 is on the Cribb Street side and is for trains travelling east. There is no overpass joining the platforms, access is at street level.

Rockhampton Station and Travel Centre.texttranscriptstring:
Rockhampton railway station is located on the southside of the city and can be accessed from Murray, George or South Streets. The station uses one platform for passenger trains travelling south to Bundaberg and Brisbane, north to Cairns and west to Longreach. There is a carpark at the front of the station, including accessible spaces, and a drop off zone closer to the entrance. 

The path to the platform is flat. You will pass through the main foyer, where the Travel Centre is on the left and the platform is straight ahead. The Travel Centre is where you may purchase long-distance rail tickets. 

From the platform there is access to the customer lounge and toilets, which have an accessible option and baby change facilities. 

There is also: 

  • customer seating and shelter
  • a drink fountain 
  • a hearing loop
  • a help phone 
  • a pay phone
  • next train information and 
  • a vending machine.

There is a bus stop and taxi rank outside the entrance to the station building.

There is no level access to the train itself from the platform. If you require assistance boarding, please inform our staff at the time of booking, and we will be happy to assist you.

Mackay Railway Station.stdet:
Station access and navigation

Access to the platform is via a ramp at either the southern or northern end of the station. Customers booked to join a service at Mackay will receive a pin code and can access the platform through the customer lounge, see below for more information. ​

Customer lounge access

This station has a customer lounge that is only accessible via pin code access. Customers joining a service from Mackay station will be sent an SMS from QR_Travel on their travel date.

Customers with this pin code will be able to access the lounge prior to their departure and use the facilities including a waiting area, toilets, taxi phone/call button, hearing loop and train timetable information.
